title: Combined suspension and adherent cell bioreactor system and related methods
abstract: A cell culture bioreactor system is provided that includes a media conditioning vessel (MCV) having a conditioning inlet into the MCV and a conditioning outlet out of the MCV, and a bioreactor vessel to hold a cell substrate having a surface for adhering cells thereto. The bioreactor vessel includes a bioreactor inlet and a bioreactor outlet. The system also includes perfusion flow circuit fluidly connecting the conditioning outlet to the bioreactor inlet and the bioreactor outlet to the conditioning inlet, and a fluid bypass configured to fluidly connect the conditioning outlet to the conditioning inlet without flowing fluid through the bioreactor vessel. The system is capable of operating both in a suspension culture mode, where the cell culture media flows through the bypass and bypasses the bioreactor vessel, and in an adherent culture mode, where the cell culture media flows through the bioreactor vessel.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed: 1. A bioreactor system for culturing cells, the bioreactor system comprising: a media conditioning vessel configured to hold a volume of cell culture media, the media conditioning vessel comprising a conditioning inlet for supplying the cell culture media to the media conditioning vessel and a conditioning outlet for removing the cell culture media from the media conditioning vessel; a bioreactor vessel configured to hold a cell substrate, the bioreactor vessel comprising a bioreactor inlet configured to supply cell culture media to the bioreactor vessel and a bioreactor outlet configured to remove cell culture media from the bioreactor vessel, the cell substrate comprising a surface configured for adhering cells thereto; a perfusion flow circuit fluidly connecting the conditioning outlet to the bioreactor inlet and the bioreactor outlet to the conditioning inlet; and a fluid bypass configured to fluidly connect the conditioning outlet to the conditioning inlet without flowing fluid through the bioreactor vessel. 2. The bioreactor system of claim 1, wherein the cell culture bioreactor system is capable of operating in both a suspension culture mode and an adherent culture mode, wherein, in the suspension culture mode, the cell culture media flows through the bypass and bypasses the bioreactor vessel, and wherein, in the adherent culture mode, the cell culture media flows through the bioreactor vessel. 3. The bioreactor system of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein a first end of the fluid bypass is connected to the perfusion flow circuit between the conditioning outlet and the bioreactor inlet. 4. The bioreactor system of any one of the preceding claims, wherein a second end of the fluid bypass is connected to the perfusion flow circuit between the bioreactor outlet and the conditioning inlet. 5. The bioreactor system of any one of claims 2-4, further comprising a bypass flow restrictor configured to stop flow through the fluid bypass in adherent culture mode. 6. The bioreactor system of claim 5, wherein the bypass flow restrictor comprises a valve or a clamp that restricts fluid flow through the fluid bypass. 7. The bioreactor system of any one of claims 2-6, further comprising a bioreactor flow restrictor configured to stop flow to the bioreactor vessel via at least one of the bioreactor inlet and the bioreactor outlet in suspension culture mode. 8. The bioreactor system of claim 7, wherein the reactor flow restrictor comprises a valve or a clamp that restricts fluid flow to the bioreactor vessel. 9. The bioreactor system of claim 7 or claim 8, wherein the bioreactor flow restrictor comprises a first flow restrictor between the bioreactor inlet and the fluid bypass, and a second flow restrictor between the bioreactor outlet and the fluid bypass. 10. The bioreactor system of any one of the preceding claims, further comprising one or more sensors for measuring a quality of the cell culture media. 11. The bioreactor system of claim 10, wherein the one or more sensors comprises a sensor is configured to measure the cell culture media while inside the media conditioning vessel. 12. The bioreactor system of claim 10, wherein the one or more sensors comprises an inline sensor in at least one of the perfusion flow circuit and the fluid bypass. 13. The bioreactor system of any one of claims 10-12, wherein the one or more sensors comprises a sensor for measuring at least one of pH, oxygen, and temperature of the cell culture media. 14. The bioreactor system of any one of the preceding claims, further comprising a pump configured to pump cell culture media through the bioreactor system. 15. The cell culture bioreactor system of claim 14, further comprising a controller configured to control at least one of a speed of the pump and activation of the suspension culture mode or the adherent culture mode. 16. The cell culture bioreactor system of claim 15, further comprising a computer- readable medium storing instructions that, when executed by a processor, cause the controller to optionally: i) open the bypass flow restrictor and close the bioreactor flow restrictor in suspension culture mode, or ii) close the bypass flow restrictor and open the bioreactor flow restrictor in adherent culture mode. 17. The cell culture bioreactor system of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the cell substrate is arranged in a fixed-bed arrangement within the bioreactor vessel. 18. The cell culture bioreactor system of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the media conditioning vessel is configured to hold different volumes of cell culture media depending on the size of the cell culture. 19. The cell culture bioreactor system of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the cell substrate comprises a structurally defined multi-layered substrate. 20. The cell culture bioreactor system of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the cell substrate comprises a physical structure and a porosity that are substantially regular and uniform. 21. The cell culture bioreactor system of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the cell substrate is configured for uniform fluid flow therethrough. 22. The cell culture bioreactor system of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the cell substrate comprises a plurality of substrate disks stacked in the bioreactor vessel. 23. The cell culture bioreactor system of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the cell substrate comprises a woven substrate having a plurality of interwoven fibers with surfaces configured for adhering cells thereto, the plurality of interwoven fibers defining a plurality of openings. 24. A method of culturing cells in a bioreactor system, the method comprising: performing cell culture in the bioreactor system in a suspension culture mode comprising: filling a media conditioning vessel with an initial suspension culture media volume; inoculating the media conditioning vessel with suspended cells; and culturing cells within the media conditioning vessel over time to grow the number of cells in the culture; and after performing the cell culture in the suspension culture mode for a period of time, switching the bioreactor system to an adherent culture mode comprising: flowing cell culture media and cells from the media conditioning vessels to a bioreactor vessel comprising a cell substrate, the cell substrate comprising a surface configured for cells to adhere to; seeding the cells onto the cell substrate; and perfusing cell culture media from the media conditioning vessel, through the bioreactor vessel, and back into the media conditioning vessel during the culture. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein performing the cell culture in the suspension culture mode further comprises: after inoculating the media conditioning vessel, increasing a volume of media in the media conditioning vessel over time to scale-up a total number of cells in the culture. 26. The method of claim 24 or claim 25, wherein culturing the cells within the media conditioning vessel during the suspension culture mode comprises perfusing media out of the media conditioning vessel, through a fluid bypass pathway, and back into the media conditioning vessel. 27. The method of any of claims 24-26, wherein the adherent culture mode further comprises performing a media exchange in the media conditioning vessel to refresh or switch media used for culturing. 28. The method of any one of claims 24-27, further comprising controlling a perfusion flow rate of the cell culture media based on one or more properties of the cell culture media or the cells. 29. The method of claim 28, wherein the one or more properties comprises pH, dissolved oxygen, temperature, or elapsed time of cell culture. 30. A method of culturing cells using the bioreactor system of any one of claims 2-23, the method comprising: performing the suspension culture mode comprising flowing cell culture media from the media conditioning vessel, through the fluid bypass, and back to the media conditioning vessel; and after a predetermined time, performing the adherent culture mode by closing flow through the fluid bypass and opening flow through the bioreactor vessel. 31. The method of claim 30, wherein the switch from perfusion culture mode to adherent culture mode is performed aseptically.
